title: Context-specific animated interaction icons
description: Replace the generic chat bubble with 5 context-specific animated 8x8 pixel icons for agent interactions. Each interaction type (planning, brainstorming, building, reviewing, auditing) gets its own icon with distinct color and animation.

scope.in: InteractionType union in types.ts, interactionMap type widening in renderer.ts/CanvasOffice.tsx, GamePage.tsx interaction pair tagging, new interactionIcons.ts sprite file, renderInteractionIcons replacing renderChatBubbles
scope.out: Status icons (working/idle/error), meeting room routing logic, review pair routing logic, modifier dots or layered signals

dod.criterion: 5 distinct interaction icons render above agent nameplates based on pipeline step type
Each icon is distinguishable by shape at 8x8 pixels
Zoom < 2 fallback shows per-type colored dots
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Define InteractionType and widen interactionMap through the data pipeline
tasks.user_scenario: No visible change yet — typed data plumbing for subsequent visual work.
tasks.scope: Add InteractionType union to types.ts. Widen IdentityOverlay.interactionMap from Map<number, number> to Map<number, {partnerId: number, type: InteractionType}>. Update agentInteractions prop from Array<[string, string]> to Array<[string, string, InteractionType]>. Update GamePage.tsx to derive InteractionType from currentStepId. Update all consumers in renderer.ts and CanvasOffice.tsx.

dod.criterion: InteractionType union type exported from types.ts with exactly 5 members: planning, brainstorming, building, reviewing, auditing
IdentityOverlay.interactionMap typed as Map<number, {partnerId: number, type: InteractionType}> and all consumers in renderer.ts destructure partnerId correctly
CanvasOffice.tsx builds the widened map from the updated agentInteractions prop and facing-direction logic uses .partnerId
GamePage.tsx tags each interaction pair with the correct InteractionType derived from currentStepId
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Create 5 interaction icon sprite sets in interactionIcons.ts
tasks.user_scenario: No visible change yet — creates the pixel art assets for the renderer.
tasks.scope: Create src/components/game/sprites/interactionIcons.ts following the expand()+palette pattern from statusIcons.ts. Define 5 sprite sets: clipboard (blue), lightbulb (amber), hammer (gray), magnifying glass (purple), shield (orange). Each 2-4 animation frames at 8x8. Export INTERACTION_SPRITES and INTERACTION_DOT_COLORS.

dod.criterion: interactionIcons.ts exists at src/components/game/sprites/ and exports INTERACTION_SPRITES and INTERACTION_DOT_COLORS
INTERACTION_SPRITES has entries for all 5 InteractionType values, each with 2-4 SpriteData frames
All sprites use the expand()+palette pattern consistent with statusIcons.ts (8x8 grid, '.' for transparent)
INTERACTION_DOT_COLORS maps each InteractionType to a distinct hex color matching the sprite palette primary
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Replace renderChatBubbles with type-aware renderInteractionIcons
tasks.user_scenario: When agents interact during a directive, the icon above their heads reflects what they are doing — clipboard for planning, lightbulb for brainstorming, hammer for building, magnifying glass for reviewing, shield for auditing.
tasks.scope: Replace renderChatBubbles in renderer.ts with renderInteractionIcons. Select sprite set by type from interactionMap, animate frames, render in same position. At zoom < 2 use per-type colored dot. Update call site.

dod.criterion: renderChatBubbles removed and replaced by renderInteractionIcons with same positioning logic
renderInteractionIcons selects sprite from INTERACTION_SPRITES based on type and animates frames
Zoom < 2 fallback uses per-type dot color from INTERACTION_DOT_COLORS
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

sequencing_rationale: Task 1 widens the type system. Task 2 creates sprite assets. Task 3 wires sprites into the renderer, depending on both.
